theorems.thm-lp-outcomes: \text{Exactly one holds: }S=\varnothing,\quad \sup_{x\in S}c^{\mathsf T}x=+\infty,\quad\text{or an optimal }x^*\in S\text{ exists.}
theorems.thm-simplex-termination-test: \bar c_N\le0\implies\bar x\text{ is optimal};\qquad \bar c_k>0,\ \bar A_k\le0\implies(P)\text{ is unbounded.}
theorems.thm-bfs-extreme: \bar x\in P=\{x:Ax=b,\ x\ge0\}:\qquad \bar x\text{ is extreme}\iff\bar x\text{ is a BFS}
theorems.thm-weak-duality: x\text{ primal feasible},\ y\text{ dual feasible}\implies c^{\mathsf T}x\le b^{\mathsf T}y
theorems.thm-strong-duality: (P)\text{ has an optimum}\iff(D)\text{ has an optimum},\qquad v(P)=v(D)
theorems.thm-farkas: \text{Exactly one holds: }\exists x\ge0:Ax=b;\qquad\exists y:A^{\mathsf T}y\ge0,\ b^{\mathsf T}y<0.
theorems.thm-kkt-course: \bar x\text{ is optimal}\iff-c\in\operatorname{cone}\{\nabla g_i(\bar x):i\in J(\bar x)\}

q-cert-unbounded.fullSolutionLatex: A\bar x=b,\quad\bar x\ge0,\quad Ad=0,\quad d\ge0,\quad c^{\mathsf T}d>0

q-weak-duality.fullSolutionLatex: c^{\mathsf T}x=12=b^{\mathsf T}y\implies x,y\text{ are optimal}

q-ip-relaxation.fullSolutionLatex: x^*\in P\cap\mathbb{Z}^n,\qquad c^{\mathsf T}x\le c^{\mathsf T}x^*\quad\forall x\in P\cap\mathbb{Z}^n
q-ip-relaxation.finalAnswerLatex: x^*\text{ is optimal for the IP}

q-kkt-assumptions.fullSolutionLatex: \begin{gathered}g_i\text{ is convex and differentiable for every }i,\\\exists x':g_i(x')<0\quad\forall i,\\g_i(\bar x)\le0\quad\forall i.\end{gathered}
